title: General wedding details
text:
Hello family,
I wanted to send an email instead of message the family group chat since emails are slightly more ‘permanent’, and you will need to refer back to this from time to time. Mom and Dad you should probably print this.
We are 90% sure that we will have our wedding on the 23rd. of January, 2027. But we will send a save the date in the next couple of days to lock this in. The venue will be our house - 23 Kererū street, Taupō, New Zealand. Seems stupid that I need to say this, but all of you are invited.
This email is just to give you some details about logistics, and what you can expect when coming all this way. I appreciate NZ is a long way to come for a wedding, especially with babies, and we want to help as much as we can to make it a smooth trip. Hopefully after visiting you will understand why we have chosen to live here.
Getting to NZ There are a 3 or so airlines that fly to New Zealand direct from U.S.A. I have flown on a few of them. We recommend Air New Zealand. They have nice planes, service, food, and offer a great option those with babies by the addition to your ticket called the “sky couch”. Air New Zealand is also a member of the Star Alliance so you can use points you have accumulated from other Star Alliance airlines (such as United).
AirNZ flies direct to Auckland NZ from Los Angles, San Fransisco, New York, Vancouver, and Houston. From Auckland you can fly to Taupō (a 50 min. flight) or perhaps if multiple of you land at the same time Ella and I can drive up to Auckland and pick you up in the car. The flight from Auckland to Taupō is less than $80 USD so that probably makes the most sense. I encourage you to send us your flights before you book them, sometimes the slightly cheaper option will send you the wrong way around the world and be much worse.
Taupō Taupō is a small town in the centre of the north island of NZ. We lived in Dunedin, Wellington, and have now landed here. Taupō is known for it’s natural beauty and tourist attractions. The natural beauty highlights include geothermal areas (this is actually what my company does), the maunga Ruapehū, and lake Taupō. Although there are many tourists, it still feels like a small town. Lake Taupō is the biggest lake in NZ, and feels a lot like lake Michigan in terms of its clarity and summer temperature. Because of the tourist industry there are many Air B&B, hotels, restaurants, and attractions. We have bungy jumping, sky diving, river jet boat rides, fly fishing, wine tasting, beaches, the list is endless really. Rather than spam you with a huge list of things to do in this email, probably best if you just look it up online. I’ll send a list of our favourite beeches, restaurants, bike trails, etc. later on.
Venue Not much to say here. It is at our house - 23 Kererū street, Two Mile Bay, Taupō, New Zealand. I think most of you have seen photos already.
Accommodation We are thinking it will be best to get a Whiting family Air B&B you can all share that is within walking distance to our house. There are a few options.
Wedding size This is going to be a pretty small event with only our closest friends and family invited. Currently there are about 45 people on the list.

I hope this can be treated as ‘forced’ vacation for all of you. The word “forced” carrying the meaning that you will feel strongly obliged to take some time of work, and treat yourself to a vacation in our beautiful little sheep overrun country. That said, reiterating I understand this is an expensive trip. If you don’t think you can afford this let me know and maybe we can work something out.

John and Ella
